attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Hot inner-loop detector wrapper (system-mode, baremetal RISC-V).
3	#
4	# Runs a baremetal ELF under qemu-system-<arch> with the hotloops plugin
5	# and prints a sorted table of detected backward branches:
6	#
7	#   iters | start_pc -> end_pc | function:offset
8	#
9	# Usage:
10	#   scripts/hotloops.sh [opts] <elf>
11	#
12	# Options:
13	#   --arch <riscv32|riscv64|auto>   Target arch (default: auto)
14	#   --qemu <path>                   Override qemu-system binary
15	#   --plugin <path>                 Override plugin .so
16	#   --machine <name>                -M <name> (default andes_ae350)
17	#   --cpu <spec>                    -cpu <spec>
18	#                                   (default andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024)
19	#   --qemu-config <file>            -readconfig <file> (e.g. AE350 device cfg)
20	#   --cwd <dir>                     chdir before launching QEMU so semihosting
21	#                                   opens (fread of bin/*.bin) resolve
22	#   --qemu-arg <arg>                Append an extra arg to the QEMU command
23	#                                   (repeatable)
24	#   --limit <N>                     Top-N loops to print (default 50)
25	#   --min-iters <N>                 Drop loops with fewer than N iters (default 1)
26	#   --objdump <path>                Override objdump (resolves symbols)
27	#   --addr2line <path>              Override addr2line (resolves source lines)
28	#   --no-source                     Disable source file:line resolution
29	#   --raw                           Print raw plugin output, no formatting
30	#   --optimize-rvv                  Only report loops with at least one RVV
31	#                                   instruction (default: on)
32	#   --no-optimize-rvv               Disable the RVV-only filter
33	#   --llm                           Emit a Markdown report tuned for LLM
34	#                                   consumption: TL;DR + per-loop fact-sheets
35	#                                   with stable IDs, source snippets, RVV
36	#                                   instruction listings, nesting, and direct
37	#                                   callees (default: on).
38	#   --no-llm                        Emit the plain human-readable table.
39	#   -h | --help                     Show help
40	
41	set -euo pipefail
42	
43	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
44	QEMU_ROOT="$(cd -- "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
45	
46	ARCH="auto"
47	QEMU=""
48	PLUGIN=""
49	LIMIT=50
50	MIN_ITERS=1
51	OBJDUMP=""
52	ADDR2LINE=""
53	NO_SOURCE=0
54	RAW=0
55	OPTIMIZE_RVV=1
56	LLM=1
57	MACHINE="andes_ae350"
58	CPU="andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024"
59	QEMU_CONFIG=""
60	QEMU_CWD=""
61	EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS=()
62	
63	usage() { sed -n '2,39p' "$0"; exit 0; }
64	
65	# --- arg parsing ---
66	ELF=""
67	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
68	    case "$1" in
69	        --arch)        ARCH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
70	        --qemu)        QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
71	        --plugin)      PLUGIN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
72	        --machine)     MACHINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
73	        --cpu)         CPU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
74	        --qemu-config) QEMU_CONFIG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
75	        --cwd)         QEMU_CWD="$2"; shift 2 ;;
76	        --qemu-arg)    EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS+=("$2"); shift 2 ;;
77	        --limit)       LIMIT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
78	        --min-iters)   MIN_ITERS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
79	        --objdump)     OBJDUMP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
80	        --addr2line)   ADDR2LINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
81	        --no-source)   NO_SOURCE=1; shift ;;
82	        --raw)         RAW=1; shift ;;
83	        --optimize-rvv)    OPTIMIZE_RVV=1; shift ;;
84	        --no-optimize-rvv) OPTIMIZE_RVV=0; shift ;;
85	        --llm)             LLM=1; shift ;;
86	        --no-llm)          LLM=0; shift ;;
87	        -h|--help)     usage ;;
88	        -*) echo "unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
89	        *)
90	            if [[ -z "$ELF" ]]; then
91	                ELF="$1"; shift
92	            else
93	                echo "error: unexpected positional arg: $1" >&2; exit 2
94	            fi ;;
95	    esac
96	done
97	
98	[[ -n "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: ELF path required" >&2; exit 2; }
99	[[ -f "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: not found: $ELF" >&2; exit 2; }
100	
101	# --- arch detection ---
102	if [[ "$ARCH" == "auto" ]]; then
103	    file_out="$(file -L -- "$ELF" 2>/dev/null || true)"
104	    case "$file_out" in
105	        *"ELF 64-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv64 ;;
106	        *"ELF 32-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv32 ;;
107	        *) echo "error: cannot auto-detect arch (not RISC-V?), pass --arch" >&2; exit 2 ;;
108	    esac
109	fi
110	
111	# --- locate qemu + plugin ---
112	if [[ -z "$QEMU" ]]; then
113	    QEMU="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/qemu-system-${ARCH}"
114	fi
115	[[ -x "$QEMU" ]] || { echo "error: qemu not found/executable: $QEMU" >&2; exit 2; }
116	
117	if [[ -z "$PLUGIN" ]]; then
118	    PLUGIN="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/contrib/plugins/libhotloops.so"
119	fi
120	[[ -f "$PLUGIN" ]] || { echo "error: plugin not built: $PLUGIN" >&2; exit 2; }
121	
122	# --- run ---
123	LOG="$(mktemp -t hotloops.XXXXXX.log)"
124	trap 'rm -f "$LOG"' EXIT
125	
126	QEMU_CMD=("$QEMU"
127	          -plugin "${PLUGIN},limit=${LIMIT},min_iters=${MIN_ITERS}"
128	          -d plugin -D "$LOG"
129	          -M "$MACHINE"
130	          -cpu "$CPU"
131	          -nographic -semihosting
132	          -bios "$ELF")
133	[[ -n "$QEMU_CONFIG" ]] && QEMU_CMD+=(-readconfig "$QEMU_CONFIG")
134	[[ ${#EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]} -gt 0 ]] && QEMU_CMD+=("${EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]}")
135	if [[ -n "$QEMU_CWD" ]]; then
136	    ( cd "$QEMU_CWD" && "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" ) >/dev/null
137	else
138	    "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" >/dev/null
139	fi
140	
141	if [[ "$RAW" -eq 1 ]]; then
142	    cat "$LOG"
143	    exit 0
144	fi
145	
146	# --- symbol table from objdump (best effort) ---
147	if [[ -z "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
148	    for cand in llvm-objdump riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump \
149	                riscv32-unknown-elf-objdump objdump; do
150	        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
151	            OBJDUMP="$cand"
152	            break
153	        fi
154	    done
155	fi
156	
157	SYMS="$(mktemp -t hotloops_syms.XXXXXX)"
158	LINES="$(mktemp -t hotloops_lines.XXXXXX)"
159	DISASM="$(mktemp -t hotloops_disasm.XXXXXX)"
160	trap 'rm -f "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$DISASM"' EXIT
161	if [[ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
162	    # Capture FUNC symbols: addr size type name
163	    "$OBJDUMP" -t "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
164	        | awk 'tolower($0) ~ /[ \t]f[ \t]/ && NF>=5 {
165	                  addr=strtonum("0x" $1);
166	                  size=strtonum("0x" $(NF-1));
167	                  name=$NF;
168	                  if (size>0) print addr, size, name;
169	              }' \
170	        | sort -n -k1,1 > "$SYMS" || true
171	
172	    # Capture disassembly: each output line is "<hex-pc> <mnemonic> <operands>".
173	    # Works for both GNU binutils objdump and llvm-objdump. Used by the RVV
174	    # filter (mnemonic) and --llm mode (operands, callee names).
175	    if [[ "$OPTIMIZE_RVV" -eq 1 || "$LLM" -eq 1 ]]; then
176	        "$OBJDUMP" -d --no-show-raw-insn "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
177	            | awk '
178	                /^[ \t]*[0-9a-fA-F]+:[ \t]+[^ \t]+/ {
179	                    line = $0;
180	                    sub(/^[ \t]+/, "", line);
181	                    sub(/[ \t]+$/, "", line);
182	                    sub(/:[ \t]+/, " ", line);
183	                    print line;
184	                }' > "$DISASM" || true
185	    fi
186	fi
187	
188	# --- locate addr2line for source line resolution (DWARF) ---
189	if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -z "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
190	    for cand in llvm-addr2line addr2line \
191	                riscv64-unknown-elf-addr2line riscv32-unknown-elf-addr2line \
192	                riscv64-linux-gnu-addr2line; do
193	        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
194	            ADDR2LINE="$cand"
195	            break
196	        fi
197	    done
198	fi
199	
200	# Pre-collect all PCs from the log and batch-resolve to file:line.
201	# LINES file format: <hex-pc> <file:line>
202	if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -n "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
203	    pcs="$(awk -F',' '/^0x/ {gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$1); gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$2); print $1; print $2}' "$LOG" | sort -u)"
204	    if [[ -n "$pcs" ]]; then
205	        # addr2line: -e <elf> reads addrs on stdin, prints file:line.
206	        # We keep full paths here (no -s) so --llm mode can locate the
207	        # source file for code snippets; table mode basenames in Python.
208	        out="$(printf '%s\n' $pcs | "$ADDR2LINE" -e "$ELF" 2>/dev/null || true)"
209	        paste <(printf '%s\n' $pcs) <(printf '%s\n' "$out") > "$LINES" || true
210	    fi
211	fi
212	
213	# --- format ---
214	python3 - "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$DISASM" "$LIMIT" "$MIN_ITERS" \
215	        "$OPTIMIZE_RVV" "$LLM" "$ELF" <<'PY'
216	import sys, os, re, bisect
217	
218	log_path     = sys.argv[1]
219	syms_path    = sys.argv[2]
220	lines_path   = sys.argv[3]
221	disasm_path  = sys.argv[4]
222	limit        = int(sys.argv[5])
223	min_iters    = int(sys.argv[6])
224	optimize_rvv = int(sys.argv[7]) == 1
225	llm_mode     = int(sys.argv[8]) == 1
226	elf_path     = sys.argv[9]
227	
228	# --- symbols (FUNC syms): list of (addr, size, name) sorted by addr ---
229	syms = []
230	try:
231	    with open(syms_path) as f:
232	        for line in f:
233	            parts = line.split()
234	            if len(parts) >= 3:
235	                syms.append((int(parts[0]), int(parts[1]),
236	                             " ".join(parts[2:])))
237	except FileNotFoundError:
238	    pass
239	
240	def resolve(pc):
241	    for addr, size, name in syms:
242	        if addr <= pc < addr + size:
243	            return f"{name}+0x{pc-addr:x}"
244	    return "?"
245	
246	def fn_of(pc):
247	    for addr, size, name in syms:
248	        if addr <= pc < addr + size:
249	            return name
250	    return None
251	
252	# --- source map: pc -> "path:line" (full path; basename in Python for table) ---
253	src_map = {}
254	try:
255	    with open(lines_path) as f:
256	        for line in f:
257	            parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t", 1)
258	            if len(parts) == 2:
259	                pc_str, src = parts
260	                src = src.strip()
261	                if src and not src.startswith("??"):
262	                    try:
263	                        src_map[int(pc_str, 16)] = src
264	                    except ValueError:
265	                        pass
266	except FileNotFoundError:
267	    pass
268	
269	def src_full(pc):
270	    return src_map.get(pc, "")
271	
272	def src_basename(s):
273	    """Basename the path part of a 'path:line[ (extra)]' string."""
274	    if not s:
275	        return ""
276	    head, sep, tail = s.partition(" ")
277	    return os.path.basename(head) + (sep + tail if sep else "")
278	
279	def src_short(pc):
280	    return src_basename(src_full(pc))
281	
282	# --- disassembly: pc -> (mnemonic, operands) ---
283	disasm = {}
284	try:
285	    with open(disasm_path) as f:
286	        for line in f:
287	            line = line.rstrip("\n")
288	            parts = line.split(None, 2)
289	            if len(parts) < 2:
290	                continue
291	            try:
292	                pc = int(parts[0], 16)
293	            except ValueError:
294	                continue
295	            mnemonic = parts[1]
296	            operands = parts[2] if len(parts) >= 3 else ""
297	            disasm[pc] = (mnemonic, operands)
298	except FileNotFoundError:
299	    pass
300	
301	disasm_pcs = sorted(disasm.keys())
302	
303	def is_rvv_mnemonic(mn):
304	    mn = mn.lower()
305	    return mn.startswith("v") and len(mn) > 1 and mn[1].isalpha()
306	
307	rvv_pcs = sorted(pc for pc, (mn, _) in disasm.items() if is_rvv_mnemonic(mn))
308	
309	def has_rvv_in_range(s, e):
310	    if not rvv_pcs or s > e:
311	        return False
312	    i = bisect.bisect_left(rvv_pcs, s)
313	    return i < len(rvv_pcs) and rvv_pcs[i] <= e
314	
315	def rvv_insns_in_range(s, e):
316	    out = []
317	    if not rvv_pcs or s > e:
318	        return out
319	    i = bisect.bisect_left(rvv_pcs, s)
320	    while i < len(rvv_pcs) and rvv_pcs[i] <= e:
321	        pc = rvv_pcs[i]
322	        mn, ops = disasm[pc]
323	        out.append((pc, mn, ops))
324	        i += 1
325	    return out
326	
327	CALL_MN = {"jal", "jalr", "c.jal", "c.jalr"}
328	CALLEE_RE = re.compile(r"<([^>]+)>")
329	
330	def calls_in_range(s, e):
331	    out = []
332	    if not disasm_pcs or s > e:
333	        return out
334	    i = bisect.bisect_left(disasm_pcs, s)
335	    while i < len(disasm_pcs) and disasm_pcs[i] <= e:
336	        pc = disasm_pcs[i]
337	        mn, ops = disasm[pc]
338	        if mn in CALL_MN:
339	            m = CALLEE_RE.search(ops)
340	            target = m.group(1) if m else "(indirect)"
341	            out.append((pc, mn, target))
342	        i += 1
343	    return out
344	
345	def parse_file_line(s):
346	    """'path:42' or 'path:42 (discriminator 1)' -> (path, 42)."""
347	    if not s or s.startswith("??"):
348	        return None, None
349	    m = re.match(r"^(.+?):(\d+)(?::\d+)?(?:\s|$)", s)
350	    if not m:
351	        return None, None
352	    return m.group(1), int(m.group(2))
353	
354	def read_source_line(path, lineno):
355	    if not path or not os.path.isfile(path):
356	        return None
357	    try:
358	        with open(path, errors="replace") as f:
359	            for i, line in enumerate(f, 1):
360	                if i == lineno:
361	                    return line.rstrip()
362	    except OSError:
363	        return None
364	    return None
365	
366	# --- raw rows from plugin ---
367	rows = []
368	with open(log_path) as f:
369	    for line in f:
370	        m = re.match(r"\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(\d+)",
371	                     line)
372	        if m:
373	            rows.append((int(m.group(1), 16),
374	                         int(m.group(2), 16),
375	                         int(m.group(3))))
376	
377	if not rows:
378	    sys.stderr.write("hotloops: no backward edges observed\n")
379	    sys.exit(0)
380	
381	total_iters_all = sum(r[2] for r in rows)
382	
383	rows = [r for r in rows if r[2] >= min_iters]
384	if optimize_rvv:
385	    rows = [r for r in rows if has_rvv_in_range(r[0], r[1])]
386	rows.sort(key=lambda r: -r[2])
387	rows = rows[:limit]
388	
389	if not rows:
390	    msg = f"hotloops: no loops with iters >= {min_iters}"
391	    if optimize_rvv:
392	        msg += " containing RVV instructions"
393	    sys.stderr.write(msg + "\n")
394	    sys.exit(0)
395	
396	# --- Loop objects + nesting (PC-range containment) ---
397	class Loop:
398	    __slots__ = ("id", "start", "end", "iters", "parent", "children")
399	    def __init__(self, idx, s, e, c):
400	        self.id = f"L{idx}"
401	        self.start = s
402	        self.end = e
403	        self.iters = c
404	        self.parent = None
405	        self.children = []
406	
407	loops = [Loop(i + 1, s, e, c) for i, (s, e, c) in enumerate(rows)]
408	
409	for loop in loops:
410	    best = None
411	    best_size = None
412	    for cand in loops:
413	        if cand is loop:
414	            continue
415	        if cand.start <= loop.start and loop.end <= cand.end:
416	            if cand.start == loop.start and cand.end == loop.end:
417	                continue  # identical range, ambiguous - skip
418	            sz = cand.end - cand.start
419	            if best is None or sz < best_size:
420	                best = cand
421	                best_size = sz
422	    loop.parent = best
423	
424	for loop in loops:
425	    if loop.parent is not None:
426	        loop.parent.children.append(loop)
427	
428	# ============================================================
429	# --- LLM mode: Markdown report with TL;DR + per-loop blocks ---
430	# ============================================================
431	if llm_mode:
432	    def pct(n):
433	        return (100.0 * n / total_iters_all) if total_iters_all else 0.0
434	
435	    def render_tldr(top):
436	        rvv_list = rvv_insns_in_range(top.start, top.end)
437	        if rvv_list:
438	            uniq = []
439	            seen = set()
440	            for _, mn, _ in rvv_list:
441	                if mn not in seen:
442	                    seen.add(mn)
443	                    uniq.append(mn)
444	            rvv_str = "RVV (" + ", ".join(uniq[:3])
445	            if len(uniq) > 3:
446	                rvv_str += f", +{len(uniq) - 3} more"
447	            rvv_str += ")"
448	        else:
449	            rvv_str = "scalar"
450	        fn = fn_of(top.start) or "?"
451	        src_s = src_short(top.start) or "?"
452	        if top.parent is None:
453	            nest_str = "top-level (no parent loop)"
454	        else:
455	            nest_str = f"nested inside {top.parent.id}"
456	        if top.children:
457	            cs = ", ".join(c.id for c in top.children)
458	            child_str = (f"and contains {len(top.children)} inner loop(s): "
459	                         f"{cs}")
460	        else:
461	            child_str = "and contains no inner loop"
462	        return (f"Hottest loop is {top.id} in `{fn}` ({src_s}), "
463	                f"{top.iters} iters = {pct(top.iters):.1f}% of total. "
464	                f"Body is {rvv_str}. It is {nest_str}, {child_str}.")
465	
466	    print("# Hotloops Report")
467	    print(f"target:           {os.path.basename(elf_path)}")
468	    print(f"total_iters:      {total_iters_all}")
469	    print(f"reported_loops:   {len(loops)}")
470	    print(f"min_iters:        {min_iters}")
471	    print(f"rvv_filter:       {'on' if optimize_rvv else 'off'}")
472	    print()
473	    print("## TL;DR")
474	    print(render_tldr(loops[0]))
475	    print()
476	    print("## Loops")
477	    for idx, loop in enumerate(loops):
478	        tag = " [hottest]" if idx == 0 else ""
479	        print()
480	        print(f"### {loop.id}{tag}  ({pct(loop.iters):.1f}% of total)")
481	        print(f"iters:        {loop.iters}")
482	        size = loop.end - loop.start
483	        print(f"pc_range:     0x{loop.start:x} .. 0x{loop.end:x}  "
484	              f"(size: {size} B)")
485	        print(f"function:     {resolve(loop.start)} -> {resolve(loop.end)}")
486	        src_s = src_full(loop.start)
487	        src_e = src_full(loop.end)
488	        if src_s and src_e and src_s == src_e:
489	            print(f"source:       {src_basename(src_s)}")
490	        else:
491	            print(f"source:       {src_basename(src_s) or '?'}"
492	                  f" -> {src_basename(src_e) or '?'}")
493	        path, lineno = parse_file_line(src_s)
494	        snippet = read_source_line(path, lineno) if path and lineno else None
495	        if snippet:
496	            snippet = snippet.strip()
497	            if len(snippet) > 200:
498	                snippet = snippet[:197] + "..."
499	            print(f"code:         {snippet}")
500	        rvv_list = rvv_insns_in_range(loop.start, loop.end)
501	        if rvv_list:
502	            print(f"rvv:          yes ({len(rvv_list)} insn"
503	                  f"{'s' if len(rvv_list) != 1 else ''})")
504	            print(f"rvv_insns:")
505	            for pc, mn, ops in rvv_list[:8]:
506	                print(f"  - 0x{pc:x}  {mn:<10s} {ops}")
507	            if len(rvv_list) > 8:
508	                print(f"  - ... ({len(rvv_list) - 8} more)")
509	        else:
510	            print(f"rvv:          no")
511	        print(f"parent:       {loop.parent.id if loop.parent else '-'}")
512	        if loop.children:
513	            cs = ", ".join(c.id for c in loop.children)
514	            print(f"children:     [{cs}]")
515	        else:
516	            print(f"children:     []")
517	        callees = calls_in_range(loop.start, loop.end)
518	        if callees:
519	            print(f"calls:")
520	            for pc, mn, target in callees[:8]:
521	                print(f"  - 0x{pc:x}  {mn} -> {target}")
522	            if len(callees) > 8:
523	                print(f"  - ... ({len(callees) - 8} more)")
524	        else:
525	            print(f"calls:        []")
526	    sys.exit(0)
527	
528	# ============================================================
529	# --- table mode (default, human-readable) ---
530	# ============================================================
531	hdr = f"# hot loops (iters >= {min_iters}"
532	if optimize_rvv:
533	    hdr += ", RVV-only"
534	hdr += f"), sorted desc, top {len(rows)}"
535	print(hdr)
536	
537	have_src = bool(src_map)
538	
539	w_iter = max(len("iters"), max(len(str(r[2])) for r in rows))
540	w_pc = 18  # 0x + 16 hex
541	w_sym = max(len("location"),
542	            max(len(f"{resolve(r[0])} -> {resolve(r[1])}") for r in rows))
543	
544	if have_src:
545	    def srcpair(s, e):
546	        a, b = src_short(s), src_short(e)
547	        if a and b and a == b:
548	            return a
549	        return f"{a or '?'} -> {b or '?'}"
550	    w_src = max(len("source"),
551	                max(len(srcpair(r[0], r[1])) for r in rows))
552	    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> "
553	          f"{'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | {'location':<{w_sym}} | source")
554	    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym + 3 + w_src))
555	    for s, e, c in rows:
556	        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
557	        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | "
558	              f"{loc:<{w_sym}} | {srcpair(s, e)}")
559	else:
560	    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> "
561	          f"{'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | location")
562	    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym))
563	    for s, e, c in rows:
564	        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
565	        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | {loc}")
566	PY
567

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Hot inner-loop detector wrapper (system-mode, baremetal RISC-V).
#
# Runs a baremetal ELF under qemu-system-<arch> with the hotloops plugin
# and prints a sorted table of detected backward branches:
#
#   iters | start_pc -> end_pc | function:offset
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/hotloops.sh [opts] <elf>
#
# Options:
#   --arch <riscv32|riscv64|auto>   Target arch (default: auto)
#   --qemu <path>                   Override qemu-system binary
#   --plugin <path>                 Override plugin .so
#   --machine <name>                -M <name> (default andes_ae350)
#   --cpu <spec>                    -cpu <spec>
#                                   (default andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024)
#   --qemu-config <file>            -readconfig <file> (e.g. AE350 device cfg)
#   --cwd <dir>                     chdir before launching QEMU so semihosting
#                                   opens (fread of bin/*.bin) resolve
#   --qemu-arg <arg>                Append an extra arg to the QEMU command
#                                   (repeatable)
#   --limit <N>                     Top-N loops to print (default 50)
#   --min-iters <N>                 Drop loops with fewer than N iters (default 1)
#   --objdump <path>                Override objdump (resolves symbols)
#   --addr2line <path>              Override addr2line (resolves source lines)
#   --no-source                     Disable source file:line resolution
#   --raw                           Print raw plugin output, no formatting
#   --optimize-rvv                  Only report loops with at least one RVV
#                                   instruction (default: on)
#   --no-optimize-rvv               Disable the RVV-only filter
#   --llm                           Emit a Markdown report tuned for LLM
#                                   consumption: TL;DR + per-loop fact-sheets
#                                   with stable IDs, source snippets, RVV
#                                   instruction listings, nesting, and direct
#                                   callees (default: on).
#   --no-llm                        Emit the plain human-readable table.
#   -h | --help                     Show help

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_ROOT="$(cd -- "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"

ARCH="auto"
QEMU=""
PLUGIN=""
LIMIT=50
MIN_ITERS=1
OBJDUMP=""
ADDR2LINE=""
NO_SOURCE=0
RAW=0
OPTIMIZE_RVV=1
LLM=1
MACHINE="andes_ae350"
CPU="andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024"
QEMU_CONFIG=""
QEMU_CWD=""
EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS=()

usage() { sed -n '2,39p' "$0"; exit 0; }

# --- arg parsing ---
ELF=""
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --arch)        ARCH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu)        QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --plugin)      PLUGIN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --machine)     MACHINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cpu)         CPU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-config) QEMU_CONFIG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cwd)         QEMU_CWD="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-arg)    EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS+=("$2"); shift 2 ;;
        --limit)       LIMIT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --min-iters)   MIN_ITERS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --objdump)     OBJDUMP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --addr2line)   ADDR2LINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --no-source)   NO_SOURCE=1; shift ;;
        --raw)         RAW=1; shift ;;
        --optimize-rvv)    OPTIMIZE_RVV=1; shift ;;
        --no-optimize-rvv) OPTIMIZE_RVV=0; shift ;;
        --llm)             LLM=1; shift ;;
        --no-llm)          LLM=0; shift ;;
        -h|--help)     usage ;;
        -*) echo "unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
        *)
            if [[ -z "$ELF" ]]; then
                ELF="$1"; shift
            else
                echo "error: unexpected positional arg: $1" >&2; exit 2
            fi ;;
    esac
done

[[ -n "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: ELF path required" >&2; exit 2; }
[[ -f "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: not found: $ELF" >&2; exit 2; }

# --- arch detection ---
if [[ "$ARCH" == "auto" ]]; then
    file_out="$(file -L -- "$ELF" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    case "$file_out" in
        *"ELF 64-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv64 ;;
        *"ELF 32-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv32 ;;
        *) echo "error: cannot auto-detect arch (not RISC-V?), pass --arch" >&2; exit 2 ;;
    esac
fi

# --- locate qemu + plugin ---
if [[ -z "$QEMU" ]]; then
    QEMU="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/qemu-system-${ARCH}"
fi
[[ -x "$QEMU" ]] || { echo "error: qemu not found/executable: $QEMU" >&2; exit 2; }

if [[ -z "$PLUGIN" ]]; then
    PLUGIN="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/contrib/plugins/libhotloops.so"
fi
[[ -f "$PLUGIN" ]] || { echo "error: plugin not built: $PLUGIN" >&2; exit 2; }

# --- run ---
LOG="$(mktemp -t hotloops.XXXXXX.log)"
trap 'rm -f "$LOG"' EXIT

QEMU_CMD=("$QEMU"
          -plugin "${PLUGIN},limit=${LIMIT},min_iters=${MIN_ITERS}"
          -d plugin -D "$LOG"
          -M "$MACHINE"
          -cpu "$CPU"
          -nographic -semihosting
          -bios "$ELF")
[[ -n "$QEMU_CONFIG" ]] && QEMU_CMD+=(-readconfig "$QEMU_CONFIG")
[[ ${#EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]} -gt 0 ]] && QEMU_CMD+=("${EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]}")
if [[ -n "$QEMU_CWD" ]]; then
    ( cd "$QEMU_CWD" && "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" ) >/dev/null
else
    "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" >/dev/null
fi

if [[ "$RAW" -eq 1 ]]; then
    cat "$LOG"
    exit 0
fi

# --- symbol table from objdump (best effort) ---
if [[ -z "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
    for cand in llvm-objdump riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump \
                riscv32-unknown-elf-objdump objdump; do
        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            OBJDUMP="$cand"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

SYMS="$(mktemp -t hotloops_syms.XXXXXX)"
LINES="$(mktemp -t hotloops_lines.XXXXXX)"
DISASM="$(mktemp -t hotloops_disasm.XXXXXX)"
trap 'rm -f "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$DISASM"' EXIT
if [[ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
    # Capture FUNC symbols: addr size type name
    "$OBJDUMP" -t "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
        | awk 'tolower($0) ~ /[ \t]f[ \t]/ && NF>=5 {
                  addr=strtonum("0x" $1);
                  size=strtonum("0x" $(NF-1));
                  name=$NF;
                  if (size>0) print addr, size, name;
              }' \
        | sort -n -k1,1 > "$SYMS" || true

    # Capture disassembly: each output line is "<hex-pc> <mnemonic> <operands>".
    # Works for both GNU binutils objdump and llvm-objdump. Used by the RVV
    # filter (mnemonic) and --llm mode (operands, callee names).
    if [[ "$OPTIMIZE_RVV" -eq 1 || "$LLM" -eq 1 ]]; then
        "$OBJDUMP" -d --no-show-raw-insn "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
            | awk '
                /^[ \t]*[0-9a-fA-F]+:[ \t]+[^ \t]+/ {
                    line = $0;
                    sub(/^[ \t]+/, "", line);
                    sub(/[ \t]+$/, "", line);
                    sub(/:[ \t]+/, " ", line);
                    print line;
                }' > "$DISASM" || true
    fi
fi

# --- locate addr2line for source line resolution (DWARF) ---
if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -z "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
    for cand in llvm-addr2line addr2line \
                riscv64-unknown-elf-addr2line riscv32-unknown-elf-addr2line \
                riscv64-linux-gnu-addr2line; do
        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            ADDR2LINE="$cand"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

# Pre-collect all PCs from the log and batch-resolve to file:line.
# LINES file format: <hex-pc> <file:line>
if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -n "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
    pcs="$(awk -F',' '/^0x/ {gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$1); gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$2); print $1; print $2}' "$LOG" | sort -u)"
    if [[ -n "$pcs" ]]; then
        # addr2line: -e <elf> reads addrs on stdin, prints file:line.
        # We keep full paths here (no -s) so --llm mode can locate the
        # source file for code snippets; table mode basenames in Python.
        out="$(printf '%s\n' $pcs | "$ADDR2LINE" -e "$ELF" 2>/dev/null || true)"
        paste <(printf '%s\n' $pcs) <(printf '%s\n' "$out") > "$LINES" || true
    fi
fi

# --- format ---
python3 - "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$DISASM" "$LIMIT" "$MIN_ITERS" \
        "$OPTIMIZE_RVV" "$LLM" "$ELF" <<'PY'
import sys, os, re, bisect

log_path     = sys.argv[1]
syms_path    = sys.argv[2]
lines_path   = sys.argv[3]
disasm_path  = sys.argv[4]
limit        = int(sys.argv[5])
min_iters    = int(sys.argv[6])
optimize_rvv = int(sys.argv[7]) == 1
llm_mode     = int(sys.argv[8]) == 1
elf_path     = sys.argv[9]

# --- symbols (FUNC syms): list of (addr, size, name) sorted by addr ---
syms = []
try:
    with open(syms_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            parts = line.split()
            if len(parts) >= 3:
                syms.append((int(parts[0]), int(parts[1]),
                             " ".join(parts[2:])))
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass

def resolve(pc):
    for addr, size, name in syms:
        if addr <= pc < addr + size:
            return f"{name}+0x{pc-addr:x}"
    return "?"

def fn_of(pc):
    for addr, size, name in syms:
        if addr <= pc < addr + size:
            return name
    return None

# --- source map: pc -> "path:line" (full path; basename in Python for table) ---
src_map = {}
try:
    with open(lines_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t", 1)
            if len(parts) == 2:
                pc_str, src = parts
                src = src.strip()
                if src and not src.startswith("??"):
                    try:
                        src_map[int(pc_str, 16)] = src
                    except ValueError:
                        pass
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass

def src_full(pc):
    return src_map.get(pc, "")

def src_basename(s):
    """Basename the path part of a 'path:line[ (extra)]' string."""
    if not s:
        return ""
    head, sep, tail = s.partition(" ")
    return os.path.basename(head) + (sep + tail if sep else "")

def src_short(pc):
    return src_basename(src_full(pc))

# --- disassembly: pc -> (mnemonic, operands) ---
disasm = {}
try:
    with open(disasm_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.rstrip("\n")
            parts = line.split(None, 2)
            if len(parts) < 2:
                continue
            try:
                pc = int(parts[0], 16)
            except ValueError:
                continue
            mnemonic = parts[1]
            operands = parts[2] if len(parts) >= 3 else ""
            disasm[pc] = (mnemonic, operands)
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass

disasm_pcs = sorted(disasm.keys())

def is_rvv_mnemonic(mn):
    mn = mn.lower()
    return mn.startswith("v") and len(mn) > 1 and mn[1].isalpha()

rvv_pcs = sorted(pc for pc, (mn, _) in disasm.items() if is_rvv_mnemonic(mn))

def has_rvv_in_range(s, e):
    if not rvv_pcs or s > e:
        return False
    i = bisect.bisect_left(rvv_pcs, s)
    return i < len(rvv_pcs) and rvv_pcs[i] <= e

def rvv_insns_in_range(s, e):
    out = []
    if not rvv_pcs or s > e:
        return out
    i = bisect.bisect_left(rvv_pcs, s)
    while i < len(rvv_pcs) and rvv_pcs[i] <= e:
        pc = rvv_pcs[i]
        mn, ops = disasm[pc]
        out.append((pc, mn, ops))
        i += 1
    return out

CALL_MN = {"jal", "jalr", "c.jal", "c.jalr"}
CALLEE_RE = re.compile(r"<([^>]+)>")

def calls_in_range(s, e):
    out = []
    if not disasm_pcs or s > e:
        return out
    i = bisect.bisect_left(disasm_pcs, s)
    while i < len(disasm_pcs) and disasm_pcs[i] <= e:
        pc = disasm_pcs[i]
        mn, ops = disasm[pc]
        if mn in CALL_MN:
            m = CALLEE_RE.search(ops)
            target = m.group(1) if m else "(indirect)"
            out.append((pc, mn, target))
        i += 1
    return out

def parse_file_line(s):
    """'path:42' or 'path:42 (discriminator 1)' -> (path, 42)."""
    if not s or s.startswith("??"):
        return None, None
    m = re.match(r"^(.+?):(\d+)(?::\d+)?(?:\s|$)", s)
    if not m:
        return None, None
    return m.group(1), int(m.group(2))

def read_source_line(path, lineno):
    if not path or not os.path.isfile(path):
        return None
    try:
        with open(path, errors="replace") as f:
            for i, line in enumerate(f, 1):
                if i == lineno:
                    return line.rstrip()
    except OSError:
        return None
    return None

# --- raw rows from plugin ---
rows = []
with open(log_path) as f:
    for line in f:
        m = re.match(r"\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(\d+)",
                     line)
        if m:
            rows.append((int(m.group(1), 16),
                         int(m.group(2), 16),
                         int(m.group(3))))

if not rows:
    sys.stderr.write("hotloops: no backward edges observed\n")
    sys.exit(0)

total_iters_all = sum(r[2] for r in rows)

rows = [r for r in rows if r[2] >= min_iters]
if optimize_rvv:
    rows = [r for r in rows if has_rvv_in_range(r[0], r[1])]
rows.sort(key=lambda r: -r[2])
rows = rows[:limit]

if not rows:
    msg = f"hotloops: no loops with iters >= {min_iters}"
    if optimize_rvv:
        msg += " containing RVV instructions"
    sys.stderr.write(msg + "\n")
    sys.exit(0)

# --- Loop objects + nesting (PC-range containment) ---
class Loop:
    __slots__ = ("id", "start", "end", "iters", "parent", "children")
    def __init__(self, idx, s, e, c):
        self.id = f"L{idx}"
        self.start = s
        self.end = e
        self.iters = c
        self.parent = None
        self.children = []

loops = [Loop(i + 1, s, e, c) for i, (s, e, c) in enumerate(rows)]

for loop in loops:
    best = None
    best_size = None
    for cand in loops:
        if cand is loop:
            continue
        if cand.start <= loop.start and loop.end <= cand.end:
            if cand.start == loop.start and cand.end == loop.end:
                continue  # identical range, ambiguous - skip
            sz = cand.end - cand.start
            if best is None or sz < best_size:
                best = cand
                best_size = sz
    loop.parent = best

for loop in loops:
    if loop.parent is not None:
        loop.parent.children.append(loop)

# ============================================================
# --- LLM mode: Markdown report with TL;DR + per-loop blocks ---
# ============================================================
if llm_mode:
    def pct(n):
        return (100.0 * n / total_iters_all) if total_iters_all else 0.0

    def render_tldr(top):
        rvv_list = rvv_insns_in_range(top.start, top.end)
        if rvv_list:
            uniq = []
            seen = set()
            for _, mn, _ in rvv_list:
                if mn not in seen:
                    seen.add(mn)
                    uniq.append(mn)
            rvv_str = "RVV (" + ", ".join(uniq[:3])
            if len(uniq) > 3:
                rvv_str += f", +{len(uniq) - 3} more"
            rvv_str += ")"
        else:
            rvv_str = "scalar"
        fn = fn_of(top.start) or "?"
        src_s = src_short(top.start) or "?"
        if top.parent is None:
            nest_str = "top-level (no parent loop)"
        else:
            nest_str = f"nested inside {top.parent.id}"
        if top.children:
            cs = ", ".join(c.id for c in top.children)
            child_str = (f"and contains {len(top.children)} inner loop(s): "
                         f"{cs}")
        else:
            child_str = "and contains no inner loop"
        return (f"Hottest loop is {top.id} in `{fn}` ({src_s}), "
                f"{top.iters} iters = {pct(top.iters):.1f}% of total. "
                f"Body is {rvv_str}. It is {nest_str}, {child_str}.")

    print("# Hotloops Report")
    print(f"target:           {os.path.basename(elf_path)}")
    print(f"total_iters:      {total_iters_all}")
    print(f"reported_loops:   {len(loops)}")
    print(f"min_iters:        {min_iters}")
    print(f"rvv_filter:       {'on' if optimize_rvv else 'off'}")
    print()
    print("## TL;DR")
    print(render_tldr(loops[0]))
    print()
    print("## Loops")
    for idx, loop in enumerate(loops):
        tag = " [hottest]" if idx == 0 else ""
        print()
        print(f"### {loop.id}{tag}  ({pct(loop.iters):.1f}% of total)")
        print(f"iters:        {loop.iters}")
        size = loop.end - loop.start
        print(f"pc_range:     0x{loop.start:x} .. 0x{loop.end:x}  "
              f"(size: {size} B)")
        print(f"function:     {resolve(loop.start)} -> {resolve(loop.end)}")
        src_s = src_full(loop.start)
        src_e = src_full(loop.end)
        if src_s and src_e and src_s == src_e:
            print(f"source:       {src_basename(src_s)}")
        else:
            print(f"source:       {src_basename(src_s) or '?'}"
                  f" -> {src_basename(src_e) or '?'}")
        path, lineno = parse_file_line(src_s)
        snippet = read_source_line(path, lineno) if path and lineno else None
        if snippet:
            snippet = snippet.strip()
            if len(snippet) > 200:
                snippet = snippet[:197] + "..."
            print(f"code:         {snippet}")
        rvv_list = rvv_insns_in_range(loop.start, loop.end)
        if rvv_list:
            print(f"rvv:          yes ({len(rvv_list)} insn"
                  f"{'s' if len(rvv_list) != 1 else ''})")
            print(f"rvv_insns:")
            for pc, mn, ops in rvv_list[:8]:
                print(f"  - 0x{pc:x}  {mn:<10s} {ops}")
            if len(rvv_list) > 8:
                print(f"  - ... ({len(rvv_list) - 8} more)")
        else:
            print(f"rvv:          no")
        print(f"parent:       {loop.parent.id if loop.parent else '-'}")
        if loop.children:
            cs = ", ".join(c.id for c in loop.children)
            print(f"children:     [{cs}]")
        else:
            print(f"children:     []")
        callees = calls_in_range(loop.start, loop.end)
        if callees:
            print(f"calls:")
            for pc, mn, target in callees[:8]:
                print(f"  - 0x{pc:x}  {mn} -> {target}")
            if len(callees) > 8:
                print(f"  - ... ({len(callees) - 8} more)")
        else:
            print(f"calls:        []")
    sys.exit(0)

# ============================================================
# --- table mode (default, human-readable) ---
# ============================================================
hdr = f"# hot loops (iters >= {min_iters}"
if optimize_rvv:
    hdr += ", RVV-only"
hdr += f"), sorted desc, top {len(rows)}"
print(hdr)

have_src = bool(src_map)

w_iter = max(len("iters"), max(len(str(r[2])) for r in rows))
w_pc = 18  # 0x + 16 hex
w_sym = max(len("location"),
            max(len(f"{resolve(r[0])} -> {resolve(r[1])}") for r in rows))

if have_src:
    def srcpair(s, e):
        a, b = src_short(s), src_short(e)
        if a and b and a == b:
            return a
        return f"{a or '?'} -> {b or '?'}"
    w_src = max(len("source"),
                max(len(srcpair(r[0], r[1])) for r in rows))
    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> "
          f"{'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | {'location':<{w_sym}} | source")
    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym + 3 + w_src))
    for s, e, c in rows:
        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | "
              f"{loc:<{w_sym}} | {srcpair(s, e)}")
else:
    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> "
          f"{'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | location")
    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym))
    for s, e, c in rows:
        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | {loc}")
PY

content.text:
Basic usage:

```
scripts/hotloops.sh [options] <elf>
```

Runs a baremetal RISC-V ELF under `qemu-system-<arch>` with the `libhotloops.so` plugin and prints the top backward-branch loops (default: LLM-friendly Markdown report, RVV-only filter on).

Prereqs:
- `build/qemu-system-riscv64` (or `-riscv32`) built
- `build/contrib/plugins/libhotloops.so` built
- An `objdump` and `addr2line` on PATH (for symbols + source lines)

Common invocations:
```
# defaults: AE350 + ax45mpv vlen=1024, LLM report, RVV-only
scripts/hotloops.sh path/to/app.elf

# plain table instead of Markdown
scripts/hotloops.sh --no-llm path/to/app.elf

# include scalar loops too
scripts/hotloops.sh --no-optimize-rvv path/to/app.elf

# top 20, drop loops with <1000 iters
scripts/hotloops.sh --limit 20 --min-iters 1000 app.elf

# raw plugin output (start_pc, end_pc, iters), no formatting
scripts/hotloops.sh --raw app.elf

# semihosting reads relative files (e.g. bin/*.bin) -> chdir first
scripts/hotloops.sh --cwd /path/to/workdir app.elf

# AE350 device cfg + extra QEMU args
scripts/hotloops.sh --qemu-config ae350.cfg --qemu-arg -d --qemu-arg in_asm app.elf
```

Key options (full list via `-h`):
- `--arch riscv32|riscv64|auto` (auto-detected from ELF)
- `--machine <name>` (default `andes_ae350`), `--cpu <spec>` (default `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024`)
- `--qemu <path>`, `--plugin <path>` to override binaries
- `--limit N` (default 50), `--min-iters N` (default 1)
- `--llm` / `--no-llm`, `--optimize-rvv` / `--no-optimize-rvv`
- `--no-source` to skip addr2line, `--raw` for unformatted output

Output is `iters | start_pc -> end_pc | function:offset | source` in table mode, or a Markdown TL;DR + per-loop fact sheets (with RVV insns, callees, nesting) in LLM mode.
